title: Source lineage for Legends of the Holy Rood: Symbols of the Passion and Cross-Poems
summary: The checked-in aha2702 XML copies University of Michigan CME item AHA2702.0001.001, a 2006 uncorrected OCR encoding of selected printed pages from Richard Morris's 1871 EETS Original Series 46 edition. The encoded selection contains thirteen main items or witness-versions and three appendix items: it retains Morris pp. 17–97 and 107–224, while excluding front matter, Old English item I, item V, and back matter. Morris directly edited distinct passages from seven Bodleian witnesses, six British Library witnesses, and the 20 May 1493 Wynkyn de Worde Golden Legend, while Harley MS 2277 and Additional MS 11748 served as collating witnesses; Additional 11748 also supplied a narrowly identified Royal-version passage and quoted apparatus. Complete public facsimiles of Ashmole 43 and the Vernon Manuscript, a complete scan of the 1871 EETS carrier, and all currently published Bodleian selected-image objects relevant to the other four Oxford witnesses are cached. Source, collation, physical-witness, facsimile, access, and rights layers remain separate.
review_notes: This is an edition-defined selection, not one homogeneous medieval work or Morris's complete volume.
Numbered items II and XIII each contain two distinct witness versions; the selection therefore has thirteen main items or witness-versions plus three appendix items.

Raw XML representation metrics are 4,807 L and 195 LG. Item XIII's Royal stream includes twenty L elements inside two apparatus notes; the metric is not a claim that all 4,807 lines belong to uninterrupted medieval base text.

The Bodleian one-image objects for Fairfax 14, Douce 126, and Douce 128 are complete downloads of provider-published selections but do not contain the target loci. The Liturg. 104 selection contains one overlapping target surface.
Negative current-image findings for British Library witnesses and U-M delivery are date-bounded to 2026-07-16.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for aha2702
entities.description: The exact XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The 343,905-byte XML contains sixteen represented content items, 4,807 L and 195 LG elements. Empty FIGURE/P structures preserve positions of printed image placeholders rather than manuscript miniatures.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AHA2702.0001.001
entities.description: A 2006 TEI-in-Libraries Level 1 electronic text made by uncorrected OCR, with no content editing and linked page images, from Morris's 1871 edition.
notes: The item retains Morris's title page and texts on printed pp. 17–97 and 107–224 but omits the volume's front matter, item I, item V, and back matter.

entities.label: Richard Morris, Legends of the Holy Rood (1871)
entities.description: The scholarly edition immediately underlying the U-M encoding. Morris prints several independent Middle English Cross traditions and witness versions, not a single uniform source text.

bibliographic.citation: Richard Morris, ed., Legends of the Holy Rood; Symbols of the Passion and Cross-Poems, Early English Text Society, Original Series 46 (London: N. Trübner & Co., 1871).
bibliographic.title: Legends of the Holy Rood; Symbols of the Passion and Cross-Poems
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 46

entities.label: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek complete digital scan of EETS OS 46
entities.description: A complete 274-canvas scan of BSB's EETS OS 46 carrier. It includes the whole Morris volume but not the Joseph of Arimathie binding mate described for the U-M source copy.
entities.reproduction_format: 274 color IIIF canvases; complete local image bundle

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Ashmole 43
entities.description: The direct base for Morris's first item-II version at ff. 63v–71v. Morris collated that text against Harley MS 2277.

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.value: First quarter of the fourteenth century

notes: Current catalog dating supersedes Morris's late-thirteenth-century estimate for normalized metadata.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete facsimile of MS Ashmole 43
entities.description: The complete 580-canvas Bodleian digital object; target ff. 63v–71v map to canvases 140–156.
entities.reproduction_format: 580 color IIIF canvases; complete local 1600-pixel bundle

entities.label: London, British Library, Harley MS 2277
entities.description: An imperfect Holy Rood witness at ff. 37v–39, used only to collate Morris's Ashmole-based version.

identifiers.scheme: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Eng. poet. a. 1 (Vernon Manuscript)
entities.description: The direct base for Morris's second item-II version at ff. 28vb–30ra and the Mary and the Cross version at ff. 315vc–316vc.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete facsimile of the Vernon Manuscript
entities.description: The complete 710-canvas Bodleian digital object. Target passages occupy two discontiguous ranges: canvases 76–79 and 517–519.
entities.reproduction_format: 710 color IIIF canvases; complete local 1600-pixel bundle

entities.label: London, British Library, Harley MS 4196
entities.description: The direct base for Morris's Rood-story excerpt, Finding of the Cross, and Exposition of the Cross at ff. 76va–81r, 149vb–151va, and 177rb–179ra.

identifiers.scheme: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.value: Fourth quarter of the fourteenth century to first quarter of the fifteenth

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Fairfax 14
entities.description: The direct base for Morris's item VI at ff. 88v–91v.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected image for MS Fairfax 14
entities.description: A provider-published one-canvas selection showing f. 59v. It is not a complete manuscript surrogate and does not contain target ff. 88v–91v.

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Douce 126
entities.description: The direct base for Morris's item IX at f. 90v–91r by the XML milestones; DIMEV identifies the indexed lyric at f. 90va.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected image for MS Douce 126
entities.description: A provider-published one-canvas opening at ff. 68v–69r. It does not contain the target locus.

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Douce 128
entities.description: The direct base for Morris's item X at f. 258r.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected image for MS Douce 128
entities.description: A provider-published one-canvas opening at ff. 163v–164r. It does not contain target f. 258r.

entities.label: Wynkyn de Worde, The Golden Legend (20 May 1493)
entities.description: The historical printed textual source for Morris's prose items XI and XII. The schema has no separate historical-print-witness type; this record does not characterize the incunable as a modern critical edition.

bibliographic.citation: Jacobus de Voragine, Legenda aurea sanctorum, sive Lombardica historia [English], trans. and adapted William Caxton (Westminster: Wynkyn de Worde, 20 May 1493), ISTC ij00150000; STC 24875; GW M11436.
bibliographic.title: The Golden Legend
bibliographic.pages: target leaves Cxxxi col. 1–Cxxxiii col. 2 and CClxviib col. 1–CClxx col. 1

notes: The physical copy used by Morris remains unidentified; no representative facsimile is asserted to be his exact source copy.

entities.label: London, British Library, Royal MS 17 A XXVII, ff. 71–97
entities.description: The first-quarter-fifteenth-century component directly underlying Morris's Royal version of the Symbols of the Passion, especially ff. 72v–81r.

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.value: First quarter of the fifteenth century

entities.label: London, British Library, Add MS 11748
entities.description: A fifteenth-century copy of the O Vernicle at ff. 144v–157. Morris collated both numbered XIII versions against it and explicitly supplied six Royal-version lines plus quoted terminal apparatus from it.

identifiers.scheme: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue

entities.label: London, British Library, Add MS 22029
entities.description: A four-leaf illustrated Arma Christi roll directly underlying Morris's second numbered XIII version in full at ff. 1r–4v.

identifiers.scheme: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.value: Second quarter of the fifteenth century

physical_description.extent: Four unfoliated leaves forming a roll
notes: The catalog describes twenty-four miniatures.

entities.label: London, British Library, Royal MS 18 A X
entities.description: The direct base for the appendix Royal Dispute at ff. 126v–130v and Festivals of the Church at ff. 130v–134v; the latter is incomplete at the end.

identifiers.scheme: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.value: First half of the fifteenth century

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Liturg. 104
entities.description: A Book of Hours directly underlying Morris's Horae de Cruce appendix selection. XML milestones cover seven discontiguous runs from f. 50r through f. 91r; DIMEV gives the indexed work more broadly as ff. 49–88.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected images for MS Liturg. 104
entities.description: A provider-published ten-canvas selection. Only f. 50v, canvas 5, overlaps the XML target; the object is not a complete manuscript surrogate.

entities.label: DIMEV 2.0-alpha.1 records relevant to aha2702
entities.description: The released DIMEV dataset used to verify incipits, constituent-work distinctions, and witness loci. The cached archive is supporting catalog data, not a reproduction of the edition-defined target selection.

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A: Duration of Copyright (April 2026)
entities.description: Current official legal guidance used only for the U.S. duration analysis of the 1493 and 1871 published textual and editorial layers. It is supporting material and contains no target text.

subject.description: The encoded selection, including retained title-page data and structural placeholders

subject.description: Numbered item II, Ashmole version, pp. 18–60

object.description: Imperfect Holy Rood text at ff. 37v–39

scope.notes: Harley MS 2277 is a collation witness, not Morris's continuous base text.

subject.description: Numbered item II, Vernon version, pp. 19–61

object.description: Cross-story excerpt within the Expanded Northern Passion, ff. 76va–81r
Finding of the Cross at ff. 149vb–151va
Exposition of the Cross at ff. 177rb–179ra
Mary and the Cross at ff. 315vc–316vc

subject.description: Numbered item XI, prose Invention of the Cross, pp. 154–160

scope.notes: The continuous Royal base is distinct from the parallel Additional MS 22029 version and from Additional MS 11748 collations and supplies.

subject.description: Numbered item XIII, Additional MS 22029 version, pp. 171–193
Both numbered XIII witness versions and their variant apparatus, pp. 170–196

scope.notes: The opening statement that readings come from Additional MS 11748 identifies collation, not a third continuous base version.

subject.description: Six bracketed Royal-version lines supplied at p. 176 and quoted terminal additions in apparatus at p. 192

object.description: The corresponding supplied and quoted text from ff. 144v–157

scope.notes: Only the six bracketed main-stream lines are a base-text supply; the terminal two plus fourteen lines remain quoted apparatus and are not silently promoted into the Royal witness text.

subject.description: Appendix Festivals of the Church, pp. 210–221

scope.notes: DIMEV's broader ff. 49–88 locus remains recorded as an unresolved foliation/content discrepancy.

object.description: Ten selected surfaces, including target-overlapping f. 50v but not the complete manuscript or complete target

rights.component: Repository XML artifact, including copied OCR, TEI markup, and repository packaging
rights.basis: The pinned CME root LICENSE uses the Unlicense text but expressly speaks of software; no evidence was found that expands it to the XML/OCR/markup payload, so that license is not transferred by inference.

rights.component: U-M OCR, XML encoding, metadata, and page-image links
rights.basis: The source header describes conditional restrictions and the current public route could not be inspected through the Cloudflare challenge; no item-specific reuse license was verified.

rights.component: 1871 published edition text, arrangement, editorial matter, and typography
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1871, far beyond the maximum U.S. statutory term for works already protected before 1978 described by current U.S. Copyright Office guidance.

rights.component: BSB digital facsimile and page-image derivatives
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest asserts No Copyright – Non-Commercial Use Only 1.0 for the image object. That statement does not authorize a commercial facsimile/POD use.

rights.component: Medieval wording represented in Morris's 1871 publication
rights.basis: The target wording was published in Morris's 1871 edition; this conclusion excludes manuscript photographs, catalog prose, and provider metadata.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian photographs and IIIF derivatives
rights.basis: The exact Digital Bodleian object attributes the photographs to Bodleian Libraries and licenses them CC BY-NC 4.0.
notes: Commercial print-on-demand use is outside the CC BY-NC grant unless separately authorized.

editorial_practices.representation: Retains title-page data and printed pp. 17–97 and 107–224; omits front matter, items I and V, and back matter
editorial_practices.description: The digital item is a discontiguous edition-defined selection, not a complete encoding of EETS OS 46.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Variant readings in Harley MS 2277 and Add MS 11748
editorial_practices.representation: Ashmole version collated against Harley MS 2277; both Symbols versions collated against Add MS 11748
editorial_practices.description: Morris distinguishes continuous bases from collating witnesses; the manifest therefore does not promote either collating manuscript to a parallel direct base.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Printed reproductions of Add MS 22029 miniatures in Morris's Symbols section
editorial_practices.representation: Empty FIGURE/P structural placeholders rather than encoded or embedded images
editorial_practices.description: The OCR encoding marks image positions but does not reproduce the printed illustrations or the manuscript miniatures; textual completeness must not be conflated with visual completeness.
editorial_practices.scope: Numbered item XIII, Additional MS 22029 version

evidence.citation: Pinned CME source XML for aha2702, UMDL AHA2702.0001.001.
evidence.locator: Whole file; TEI header and BODY hierarchy
evidence.summary: Confirms the artifact identity, 2006 U-M uncorrected-OCR workflow, edition source, discontiguous printed-page selection, manuscript milestones, sixteen represented items, and raw 4,807 L / 195 LG metrics.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME root LICENSE at pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Contains the Unlicense text and explicitly refers to software. It does not supply evidence for broadening that dedication to the XML/OCR/markup corpus payload.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, CME item AHA2702.0001.001.
evidence.summary: Canonical item identity. Anonymous checks reached a Cloudflare challenge on 2026-07-16, so current item-specific delivery and reuse terms could not be verified from the live interface.

evidence.citation: Richard Morris, ed., Legends of the Holy Rood, EETS Original Series 46 (London, 1871), Bayerische Staatsbibliothek bsb10748436, complete IIIF scan.
evidence.locator: Title page, preface, pp. 17–97, 107–224, and source/apparatus notes on pp. 170, 176, and 192
evidence.summary: Confirms the edition, complete 274-canvas carrier, source assignments, direct-base versus collation distinctions, Golden Legend leaves, Add MS 11748 supplied passages, Add MS 22029 illustrations, appendix boundaries, and exact encoded page ranges.
notes: All 274 bundled images, inventory records, and ZIP members were integrity-audited; the BSB carrier is OS 46 alone and not the U-M copy's larger binding.

evidence.citation: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek IIIF rights metadata for bsb10748436.
evidence.summary: The exact image manifest asserts No Copyright – Non-Commercial Use Only 1.0 for the digital image object.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS Ashmole 43 catalog and complete Digital Bodleian object.
evidence.summary: Confirms the current shelfmark, date and catalog description, complete public facsimile, exact target canvases, and object-level CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS Eng. poet. a. 1 (Vernon Manuscript) catalog and complete Digital Bodleian object.
evidence.locator: 710 canvases; target ranges at canvases 76–79 and 517–519
evidence.summary: Confirms the witness description, two target loci, complete public facsimile, exact target ranges, and object-level CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS Fairfax 14 catalog and one-image Digital Bodleian selection.
evidence.locator: Published selection f. 59v; target ff. 88v–91v absent
evidence.summary: Confirms the witness identity and target locus from the catalog, and verifies that the complete provider-published one-image object does not reproduce the target.

evidence.locator: Published opening ff. 68v–69r; target ff. 90v–91r absent
evidence.summary: Confirms the witness identity and verifies that the complete provider-published one-image object does not reproduce the target.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS Douce 128 catalog and one-image Digital Bodleian selection.
evidence.locator: Published opening ff. 163v–164r; target f. 258r absent
evidence.summary: Confirms the witness identity and verifies that the complete provider-published one-image object does not reproduce the target.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS Liturg. 104 catalog and ten-image Digital Bodleian selection.
evidence.locator: Ten selected canvases; target-overlapping f. 50v at canvas 5
evidence.summary: Confirms the witness identity and ten-canvas selected object; only f. 50v overlaps the target, so the cached provider selection is complete while target coverage remains partial.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian Terms of Use and exact-object rights metadata.
evidence.summary: The exact aha2702-related objects attribute photographs to Bodleian Libraries and state CC BY-NC 4.0; the noncommercial limitation excludes for-profit POD without separate permission.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special Collections copying and Imaging Services order routes.
evidence.summary: Supplies current paid/request-based routes for unpublished manuscript images and complete-surrogate enquiries, subject to preservation and service feasibility.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 2277, record 040-002048108.
evidence.summary: Confirms the c. 1300 parchment codex, extent, identity, and unavailable legacy digitized-content route; Morris and DIMEV identify the collating Holy Rood locus at ff. 37v–39.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 4196, record 040-002050033.
evidence.summary: Confirms the 1375–1425 parchment codex and extent; Morris and DIMEV locate the three directly edited target passages at ff. 76va–81r, 149vb–151va, and 177rb–179ra.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Royal MS 17 A XXVII, ff. 71–97, component record 041-003475038.
evidence.summary: Confirms the exact first-quarter-fifteenth-century component and its Arma Christi sequence. Catalog history reports digitization, but current anonymous image delivery was not found.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 11748, record 032-002109534.
evidence.summary: Confirms the fifteenth-century vellum small-quarto codex and Crucifixion verse content; Morris and DIMEV establish its O Vernicle locus and collation/supply roles.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 22029, record 032-002034491.
evidence.summary: Confirms the second-quarter-fifteenth-century four-leaf Arma Christi roll, dimensions, full O Vernicle text, twenty-four miniatures, Morris pp. 170–196 edition reference, and unavailable legacy image route.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Royal MS 18 A X, record 040-002107426.
evidence.summary: Confirms the first-half-fifteenth-century vellum codex, Royal Dispute and incomplete Festivals loci, and extent.

evidence.citation: British Library digitisation services.
evidence.summary: Describes the current fee-based route for reference and reproduction-quality imaging, subject to preservation and feasibility, and links the online order form.

evidence.citation: British Library Imaging Services Terms and Conditions, updated January 2026.
evidence.summary: Requires written permission before copying or reproducing supplied material. Exact rights and permissions for future ordered files remain unknown until an order is fulfilled.

evidence.citation: CERL Incunabula Short Title Catalogue, ij00150000, English Golden Legend printed by Wynkyn de Worde, 20 May 1493.
evidence.summary: Confirms title, Westminster printer and date, Caxton attribution, and bibliographic identifiers STC 24875 and GW M11436.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, release v2.0-alpha.1 (25 June 2026), records relevant to the aha2702 constituent texts and witnesses.
evidence.summary: Confirms incipit-based identifications and modern witness loci, while exposing that some DIMEV subwork loci are narrower than Morris's edition-item boundaries. CFF metadata declares CC BY 4.0.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright (April 2026).
evidence.locator: Four-page official PDF; duration table and pre-1978 term discussion
evidence.summary: Provides current official U.S. duration guidance supporting the public-domain conclusion for material published in 1493 and 1871.
notes: pdfinfo reports four pages and an April 2026 creation date. qpdf succeeds with non-fatal linearization hint-table warnings, including shared-object and page object-count mismatches.

evidence.citation: 17 U.S.C. § 105, Subject matter of copyright: United States Government works.
evidence.summary: Provides that copyright protection under Title 17 is not available for works of the United States Government, supporting the Circular's federal-government-work status.

open_questions.question: Which physical copy of the 20 May 1493 Golden Legend did Morris transcribe?
next_steps: Inspect Morris's papers or EETS production records and compare distinctive readings against extant copies.

open_questions.question: How should Morris's description of the 1493 printing as the third edition be reconciled with current catalog numbering?
next_steps: Consult current Caxton and Wynkyn de Worde bibliographies while preserving Morris's wording as attributed history of scholarship.

open_questions.question: Why do the XML milestones run through f. 91r while DIMEV gives ff. 49–88 for Horae de Cruce?
next_steps: Inspect or order all target surfaces in MS Liturg. 104 and reconcile historical and modern foliation.

open_questions.question: Does Morris's statement of seventeen couplets reflect a missing or merged line where the XML has 33 L elements?
next_steps: Collate printed pp. 222–224 against the manuscript surfaces and inspect XML line-container boundaries.

open_questions.question: Why does the British Library description refer to an incomplete ending at line 333 while the XML has 335 L elements?
next_steps: Inspect Royal MS 18 A X ff. 130v–134v and compare headings, catchwords, and Morris's lineation.

open_questions.question: Should Morris's late-thirteenth-century dating of Ashmole 43 be retained alongside the current Bodleian first-quarter-fourteenth-century date?
next_steps: Retain both with attribution and use the current catalog date for normalized witness metadata.

open_questions.question: Can current public image delivery be restored for Royal MS 17 A XXVII or Add MS 22029, both historically reported as digitized?
next_steps: Ask British Library digitisation services whether existing images can be re-delivered before commissioning new photography.
